meta.title: Parallels-graph trio: commentarial-load vs parallel-density, relation-type stratigraphy, and coverage cartography

results.label: A0_root_universe_per_sutta
results.sql: -- Sub-study (A) unit table: one row per MULA root sutta (SC fine rows, deduped), with root_chars and tag-richness. Restrict to SC edition to get per-sutta granularity (CST is coarse vagga rows). DN/MN/SN/AN kept separate via collection prefix; NEVER pooled downstream. WITH root AS ( SELECT p.id, p.work_slug, char_length(p.original) AS root_chars, CASE WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' THEN 'DN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' THEN 'MN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' THEN 'SN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'an%' THEN 'AN' ELSE 'OTHER' END AS coll FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug <> 'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true AND stratum(p.work_slug) IN ('1early','2late') ) SELECT r.id, r.work_slug, r.coll, r.root_chars, (SELECT count(*) FROM passage_tags t WHERE t.passage_id=r.id) AS tag_richness FROM root r WHERE r.coll IN ('DN','MN','SN','AN') ORDER BY r.coll, r.work_slug;

results.label: A1_par_density_per_root
results.sql: -- Per-root parallel density numerator: non-pli (foreign) parallel edges per root. Joined per-Mchar downstream against A0.root_chars. relation_type retained for later mix. SELECT pp.passage_id, count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang <> 'pli') AS foreign_par_edges, count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang = 'pli') AS pli_par_edges, count(*) AS all_edges, count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.relation_type='parallels') AS rt_parallels, count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.relation_type='retells') AS rt_retells, count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.relation_type='mentions') AS rt_mentions FROM passage_parallels pp WHERE pp.passage_id IN ( SELECT p.id FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true AND stratum(p.work_slug) IN ('1early','2late') AND (p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'an%')) GROUP BY pp.passage_id;

results.label: A2_comm_load_per_root
results.sql: -- Per-root commentarial load: attha chars whose commentary maps to this root sutta. Mapping is via shared work_slug base (mula 'mn1' <-> attha 'mn1-attha' / 'pli-vism' excluded as it is whole-work). comm_load = attha_chars / root_chars computed downstream. SELECT base.root_id, base.work_slug AS root_slug, COALESCE(SUM(char_length(a.original)),0) AS attha_chars, count(a.id) AS attha_passages FROM ( SELECT p.id AS root_id, p.work_slug, regexp_replace(p.work_slug,'^([a-z]+[0-9]+).*$','\1') AS slug_key FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true AND stratum(p.work_slug) IN ('1early','2late') AND (p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'an%') ) base LEFT JOIN passages a ON a.work_role='attha' AND a.is_primary=true AND regexp_replace(a.work_slug,'^([a-z]+[0-9]+).*$','\1') = base.slug_key GROUP BY base.root_id, base.work_slug;

results.label: A3_comm_method_density_attha
results.sql: -- Sub-study (A) content co-pass: COMM_METHOD lexicon density per Mchar in attha layer (denominator attha Mchar = 29.488533). Confirms high-comm_load attha actually does exegesis. Hits returned per work_slug for the qualitative pass; magnitude always per-Mchar. SELECT 'COMM_METHOD' AS lexicon, count(*) FILTER (WHERE a.original ~* '(\mvevacan|\mpariyāy|\mpariyay|\mnirutti|\mnibbacan|\matthuddhār|\matthuddhar|\mpadabhājan|\mpadabhajan|\mvibhaṅg|\mvibhang|\madhippāy|\madhippay|\mlakkhaṇ|\mlakkhan)') AS hit_passages, SUM(char_length(a.original)) AS attha_total_chars, round( count(*) FILTER (WHERE a.original ~* '(\mvevacan|\mpariyāy|\mpariyay|\mnirutti|\mnibbacan|\matthuddhār|\matthuddhar|\mpadabhājan|\mpadabhajan|\mvibhaṅg|\mvibhang|\madhippāy|\madhippay|\mlakkhaṇ|\mlakkhan)')::numeric / 29.488533, 3) AS hits_per_Mchar_attha FROM passages a WHERE a.work_role='attha' AND a.is_primary=true;

results.label: A4_neg_register_density_mula
results.sql: -- NEGATIVE CONTROL pass: generic-register lexicon density per mula Mchar (denominator 53.543921) and DHAMMA_EPITHET prestige density, side by side, for the (A) artifact check. If par_density tracks NEG_REGISTER as strongly as DHAMMA_EPITHET, (A) is a length/register artifact. SELECT lex, hit_passages, round(hit_passages::numeric / 53.543921, 3) AS hits_per_Mchar_mula FROM ( SELECT 'NEG_REGISTER' AS lex, count(*) FILTER (WHERE p.original ~* '(\mevaṃ\M|\mevam\M|\mtena\M|\msamayena|\mbhagavā\M|\mbhagava\M|\matha\M|\mkho\M|\mahosi|\mvuccati)') AS hit_passages FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true UNION ALL SELECT 'DHAMMA_EPITHET', count(*) FILTER (WHERE p.original ~* '(\msvākkhāt|\msvakkhat|\msandiṭṭhik|\msanditthik|\makālik|\makalik|\mehipassik|\mopaneyyik|\mopanayik|\mpaccattaṃ|\mpaccatta)') FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true ) s;

results.label: B1_retellshare_by_collection
results.sql: -- Sub-study (B): RetellShare = (retells+mentions)/all-edges, per collection, stratum-fixed to 1early, on MULA roots only, with edge-count and char totals so it can be length-controlled downstream. SN parallels-dominance read from rt_parallels share. WITH e AS ( SELECT CASE WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' THEN 'DN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' THEN 'MN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' THEN 'SN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'an%' THEN 'AN' END AS coll, pp.relation_type, char_length(p.original) AS rc FROM passages p JOIN passage_parallels pp ON pp.passage_id=p.id WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true AND stratum(p.work_slug)='1early' AND (p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'an%') ) SELECT coll, count(*) AS all_edges, count(*) FILTER (WHERE relation_type IN ('retells','mentions')) AS retell_mention_edges, count(*) FILTER (WHERE relation_type='parallels') AS parallels_edges, round(count(*) FILTER (WHERE relation_type IN ('retells','mentions'))::numeric / NULLIF(count(*),0),4) AS retell_share, round(count(*) FILTER (WHERE relation_type='parallels')::numeric / NULLIF(count(*),0),4) AS parallels_share, SUM(DISTINCT rc) AS approx_char_basis FROM e WHERE coll IS NOT NULL GROUP BY coll ORDER BY retell_share DESC;

results.label: B2_retellshare_length_control_bins
results.sql: -- Sub-study (B) length control: same RetellShare split into per-collection root-length terciles, so the DN>MN>{SN,AN} ordering can be checked WITHIN comparable length bins (defends against 'DN just has long suttas' artifact / H0-B). WITH base AS ( SELECT p.id, char_length(p.original) AS rc, CASE WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' THEN 'DN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' THEN 'MN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' THEN 'SN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'an%' THEN 'AN' END AS coll FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true AND stratum(p.work_slug)='1early' AND (p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'an%') ), binned AS ( SELECT id, coll, ntile(3) OVER (PARTITION BY coll ORDER BY rc) AS len_tercile FROM base ) SELECT b.coll, b.len_tercile, count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.relation_type IN ('retells','mentions')) AS rm_edges, count(pp.*) AS all_edges, round(count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.relation_type IN ('retells','mentions'))::numeric / NULLIF(count(pp.*),0),4) AS retell_share FROM binned b LEFT JOIN passage_parallels pp ON pp.passage_id=b.id GROUP BY b.coll, b.len_tercile ORDER BY b.coll, b.len_tercile;

results.label: C1_coverage_cartography_by_stratum
results.sql: -- Sub-study (C): per stratum, external-parallel COVERAGE (share of mula passages with >=1 foreign edge), DENSITY (foreign edges per stratum Mchar), BREADTH (distinct parallel_lang per stratum), RELATION-MIX. Per-character throughout (T2). Stratum is a lookup label (T1): never read a layer disagreement as independent chronology. WITH m AS ( SELECT p.id, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, char_length(p.original) AS rc FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true ) SELECT m.strat, count(DISTINCT m.id) AS mula_passages, count(DISTINCT m.id) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang <> 'pli') AS passages_with_foreign, round(count(DISTINCT m.id) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli')::numeric / NULLIF(count(DISTINCT m.id),0),4) AS foreign_coverage, SUM(m.rc)/1000000.0 AS strat_Mchar, round(count(pp.*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli')::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(DISTINCT m.rc)/1000000.0,0),3) AS foreign_edges_per_Mchar, count(DISTINCT pp.parallel_lang) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli') AS lang_breadth FROM m LEFT JOIN passage_parallels pp ON pp.passage_id=m.id WHERE m.strat IN ('1early','2late') GROUP BY m.strat ORDER BY m.strat;

results.label: C2_NEGCONTROL_pli_only_coverage
results.sql: -- LEAD NEGATIVE CONTROL for (C): the SAME coverage cartography but on the PLI-ONLY (intra-Pali) sub-graph. The early>late ordering must NOT be reproduced here. If intra-Pali coverage shows the same early>late gradient, the (C) effect is SC cataloguing-completeness artifact, not transmission history (H0-C wins). WITH m AS ( SELECT p.id, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, char_length(p.original) AS rc FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true ) SELECT m.strat, count(DISTINCT m.id) AS mula_passages, count(DISTINCT m.id) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang='pli') AS passages_with_pli, round(count(DISTINCT m.id) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang='pli')::numeric / NULLIF(count(DISTINCT m.id),0),4) AS pli_coverage, round(count(pp.*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang='pli')::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(DISTINCT m.rc)/1000000.0,0),3) AS pli_edges_per_Mchar FROM m LEFT JOIN passage_parallels pp ON pp.passage_id=m.id WHERE m.strat IN ('1early','2late') GROUP BY m.strat ORDER BY m.strat;

results.label: C3_sc_mapping_completeness_bound
results.sql: -- (C) SC-mapping-completeness bound: edition double-ingest check. Counts distinct works/suttas per stratum under SC (fine per-sutta) vs CST (coarse vagga) so coverage denominators are honest. A coverage 'gap' that is really a cataloguing-granularity gap gets flagged here, NOT called an interpolation. SELECT stratum(work_slug) AS strat, count(*) FILTER (WHERE source_edition='sc') AS sc_rows, count(*) FILTER (WHERE source_edition='cst') AS cst_rows, count(DISTINCT work_slug) FILTER (WHERE source_edition='sc') AS sc_distinct_works, count(DISTINCT work_slug) FILTER (WHERE source_edition='cst') AS cst_distinct_works FROM passages WHERE work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' GROUP BY stratum(work_slug) ORDER BY strat;

results.label: C4_coverage_label_disagreement_rows
results.sql: -- (C) ROWS TO FLAG FOR FOLLOW-UP (not interpolations): mula passages whose stratum lookup and whose external-coverage status disagree with neighbors in the same work (e.g. zero-coverage 1early suttas, or 2late suttas with high foreign breadth). Surface for human follow-up; do NOT auto-interpret. SELECT p.work_slug, p.id, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, char_length(p.original) AS rc, count(pp.*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli') AS foreign_edges, count(DISTINCT pp.parallel_lang) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli') AS langs FROM passages p LEFT JOIN passage_parallels pp ON pp.passage_id=p.id WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true AND stratum(p.work_slug) IN ('1early','2late') GROUP BY p.work_slug, p.id HAVING (stratum(p.work_slug)='1early' AND count(pp.*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli')=0) OR (stratum(p.work_slug)='2late' AND count(DISTINCT pp.parallel_lang) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli') >= 3) ORDER BY p.work_slug, foreign_edges DESC;

results.label: CODE_pariyaya_sense_rows
results.sql: -- POLYSEMY SENSE-AUDIT rows for COMM_METHOD: pariyāya is the lesson-of-%carita%; pull a blind sample of attha+mula hits with surrounding text for κ-coded sense split (exegetical-method/synonym vs discourse-mode/occasion) BEFORE any COMM_METHOD count is reported as 'systematization'. SELECT p.id, p.work_role, p.work_slug, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, substring(p.original from '.{0,80}(\mpariyāy|\mpariyay).{0,80}') AS kwic FROM passages p WHERE p.is_primary=true AND p.original ~* '(\mpariyāy|\mpariyay)' AND p.work_role IN ('mula','attha') ORDER BY random() LIMIT 200;
